name: fct_orders
description: Order fact table at one row per order, joining staged orders to the customer dimension.
rationale: Grain is one row per order_id; columns mirror stg_orders with a normalised amount and a foreign key to dim_customers.

columns.name: order_id
columns.description: Surrogate primary key for the order.
columns.rationale: order_id is unique in stg_orders and propagates unchanged into fct_orders.

tests.rationale: Primary keys must never be null.
One row per order_id is the documented grain.

columns.name: customer_id
columns.rationale: Every order is attributed to exactly one customer via the staging join.

tests.rationale: Customer attribution is mandatory for downstream rollups.
Referential integrity to the customer dimension.

columns.name: amount
columns.description: Order total in dollars (cents converted via /100.0).
columns.rationale: Renormalises the staged cents amount into a numeric dollars column for analytics consumers.

columns.name: ordered_at
columns.description: Timestamp the order was placed, in UTC.
columns.rationale: Carried through from stg_orders.ordered_at; load-bearing for the mart_orders_summary rollup.

tests.rationale: Sample fixture asserts the column is one of the seeded test dates.
Re-asserts the per-order grain at the model level for explicit documentation.

tests.sql: SELECT order_id FROM {{ this }} WHERE amount < 0
tests.rationale: Business rule: an order total may never be negative.
